observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Municipal Accountant agreed to:
record in the books of accounts all the disallowances with NFD and COE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006;
prepare the Bank Reconciliations Statements monthly, and submit the same to the Auditor for verification pursuant to Section 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011; and
make necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
evaluate the need for those unexpended fund balance from NGAs. Should the funds be proven no longer needed, it should be remitted to the source agencies as required under existing rules and regulations. If still needed, Management should request authority from the source agency to use the money for other related projects of the LGU;
provide individual Subsidiary Ledger of each fund transfer to determine the balances of each account since fund transfer have different source and purpose; and
Cause the liquidation of all fund transfer for which the purpose were served.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Bids and Awards Committee to undertake the preliminary examination of the bids in accordance with Section 30 of the IRR of RA No. 9184, conduct post qualification in accordance with Section 34 of the IRR, and cause the proper documentation of the evaluation process;
Ensures that Invitation to Bid, Notices of Award, Notices to Proceed and the approved contracts are posted in the PhilGEPS and website of the agency, in addition to posting of the Invitation to Bid at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose by the agency; and
Invitation to three (3) observers in writing be sent at least five (5) calendar days before procurement activity.

We recommended that:
the Municipal Mayor administer the optimal utilization of the LDRRM Fund to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to disaster and climate change impacts;
the Municipal Mayor initiate the creation of the two additional plantilla positions and fill up of the same to manage and operate the local LDRRM Office in compliance with the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01 dated April 4, 2014; and
the LDRRM Officer to prepare an LDRRM Plan with full amount of the LDRRM Fund in the Annual budget and include unexpended balances from previous years to consider current vulnerabilities and to prioritize their utilization in the ensuing year.

adopt standard and clear policies to ensure that issuance of electronic gadgets is within the bounds of necessity and responsiveness to the exigencies of the service and economy in the interest of the government; and
evaluate the reasonableness of the cellphone granted to the officials and employees and limit the issuance of cellular phones to qualified employees which should not be lower than Division Chief rank.

formulates policy guidelines in assessing the necessity of hiring job order employees taking into consideration the reasonable number needed by the different offices and the validity of work to be assigned; and

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct the inventory-taking pursuant to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and strictly comply with the same for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers/supporting schedules of all accounts in the financial statements to facilitate review and analysis of accounts and establish accuracy of their balances.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record disallowances in the books of accounts based on the NFDs received in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006.
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group  procure drugs, medicines and laboratory supplies in accordance with the Suggested Retail Price (SRP) of the DOH.
We recommended that the focal person (MSWDO) strictly adhere with applicable guidelines issued in the release of financial assistance to intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) consolidate the procurement of similar or related items, with dates of requisition or request in close proximity to one another, into one purchase request for appropriate mode of procurement, to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process.

We also recommended that the BAC Secretariat indicate all the needed data/information in the Annual Procurement Plan in accordance with Section 7 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that:

Management see to it that all claims are duly supported with complete documents in accordance with Section4 (6) of PD 1445.
The BAC  strictly observe the eligibility requirements for the procurement of goods and services;
The BAC consolidate requisitions for procurement of meals and snacks weekly or monthly for public bidding.
The General Service Officer (GSO) issue Property Acknowledgement Receipt to all end users of procured equipment.
The Secretariat of the Bids and Award Committee post the Request for Quotation and all awards in the PhilGEPS website and website of the Municipality in addition to posting at any conspicuous place in the premises of the municipality reserved for this purpose.

We recommended that the LCE require the property officer to serve notice and furnish copies of delivery documents to the Auditor's office within 24 hours from acceptance or delivery of items procured.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  verify and ascertain that payments for fuel are duly supported with completely filled up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation on the reasonableness of fuel consumption.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
require the concerned employees/officials to prepare the monthly report of official travels and the monthly report of fuel consumption in accordance with COA Circular 77-61;
require the use of Requisition and Issue Voucher (RIV) for the issuance of fuel for the generator and indicate the purpose/detail of usage of the generator in the monthly report; and
apply direct payment for the fuel procurement instead of reimbursement basis.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other concerned personnel strictly comply with Section 338 of RA 7160 prohibiting the payment of any contract under which no services have been rendered or goods delivered.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive :

enforce the COA Order of Execution (COE) by requiring the withholding of salaries or other compensation due to the person liable in satisfaction of the disallowance; and
ensure that suspensions are settled within the prescribed period.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain complete records of schedules for NDs showing the outstanding balance of each liable person on a quarterly basis.

We recommended that: 

a) The Municipal Accountant maintain Supplies/Property Ledgers, as applicable, and necessary Subsidiary Ledgers for each of the other accounts; and

b) The General Services Officer/Property Custodian maintain Supplies/Property Stock Cards; submit to the Municipal Accountant the Report on Supplies and Materials Issued as basis for recording of expenses in the books, and reconcile balances with the accounting records on a regular basis as necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should:

(a)	prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) and submit to the audit team within ten days from receipt of the monthly Bank Statement together with the paid checks, original copies of Debit Memoranda (DM)/Credit Memoranda (CM) from the Government Servicing Bank (GSB); and

(b)	effect necessary adjusting entries to eliminate the reconciling items identified in the BRS.

We recommended that Management :

a)	Require the Bids and Awards Committee to undertake the preliminary examination of the bids in accordance with Section 30 of the IRR of RA 9184, conduct post qualification in accordance with Section 34 of the IRR, and cause the proper documentation of the evaluation process; and

b)	Ensure that Invitation to Bid, Notices of Award, Notices to Proceed and the approved contracts are posted in the PhilGEPS and website of the agency, in addition to posting of the Invitation to Bid at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose by the LGU.

We recommended that Management: 
a)	impose liquidated damages for the delay incurred in the implementation of projects in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of Republic Act. No. 9184; and

b)	consider rescission or termination of the contract once liquidated damages exceed 10% of the contract amount.

We recommended that Management require contractors of awarded projects to immediately post the signboard/tarpaulin at the location of the project location following the dimensions and showing the information prescribed under COA Circular No. 2013-004

We recommended that the Local Risk Management Officer  prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day of the following month.

We recommended that:

a)	management advise the Barangay Treasurers to comply strictly with the submission of financial transaction documents to the Municipal accountant within 10 days after end of the month and furnish the audit team with copy of the duly receipted transmittal letter, together with copies of the PBCs, within three (3) working days from submission; and

b)	the Municipal Accountant ensure the recording in the barangay books of accounts and submit to the audit team, the financial transaction documents within 10 working days from receipt thereof, otherwise, inform in writing the SA/ATL of the barangay’s failure to submit.

We recommended that management comply with documentary requirements prescribed under Executive Order No. 49 on the mandatory use of PNDF, Volume I, and Item 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012 concerning purchases of drugs, medicines and laboratory supplies.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person support its GAD accomplishment report with detailed breakdown of expenditures and include in the plan performance indicators to determine propriety of the use of GAD funds and whether the gender issues identified have been effectively addressed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare analysis of PPE accounts to determine the corresponding depreciation of all depreciable assets and effect the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of PPE accounts.

We have recommended that: 

a. The Municipal Accountant record cost of issuances and balance of inventories using the Weighted Average Method in accordance with PPSAS No. 12; and

b. The General Service Officer conduct inventory taking of supplies carried at stock at least every semester for reconciliation with the balance in the books.

We have recommended that: 

a. The Local Chief Executive create an Appraisal Committee to provide necessary fair market value of all municipal local road networks; 

b. The Inventory Committee complete its inventory taking of municipal local road network, coordinate with the Appraisal Committee to provide the needed appraisal or fair market value, and report thereof shall be submitted immediately to the accounting department for appropriate recording; and 

c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer maintain subsidiary ledgers and property ledger cards, respectively, of all local road networks starting from the result of inventory taking by the Municipal Inventory Committee and the Appraisal Committee which shall be regularly updated.

We have recommended that: 

a. the Municipal Engineer conduct thorough project monitoring to ensure that infrastructure projects are completed within the contract duration and request for extension/suspension, if any, is properly evaluated to warrant granting thereof; and 

b. the Local Chief Executive consider rescission of the contract once the accumulated liquidated damages exceed the required 10% of the contract cost without prejudice to other courses of action and remedies available as required under Section 68 of the Republic Act 9184.

We have reiterated that: 

a. the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, the Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned fast track the implementation of all PPAs appropriated under the Local Development Fund including those remaining in prior years to ensure optimum utilization of the fund and timely delivery of basic services to the constituents; and 

b. the Local Chief Executive initiate the utilization of savings from completed projects for needed augmentation of the Municipal Development Fund or identification of other priority projects for funding to be covered by an appropriation ordinance.

We have recommended that: 

- the Local Chief Executive, as the Chairman of the LDRRM Council, 

a. establish clear policies/guidelines on the stockpiling and distribution of Welfare Goods for Distribution considering the expiry dates of food items or medicines; and 

b. identify storage room which is secured and suitable for Welfare Goods for Distribution carried at stock to prevent possible spoilage/damage or loss and to ensure that the same goods are readily available in their good condition in times of calamities.

We have recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board develop operating guidelines for the Material Recovery Facility to ensure efficient and effective operation of the same upon its completion.

We have recommended that: 

a. The Sangguniang Bayan prioritize the passing of an Appropriation Ordinance to authorize the disbursement made for the Investment in bonds amounting to ₱10,000,000.00 subject to the approval of the LCE and review by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor being the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) and the Municipal Treasurer or the General Services Office (GSO) - Designate consider procurement of regular or ordinary office supplies from PS-DBM unless the same are not available at the PS Depot in which case a CNAS shall be secured before procuring thru competitive bidding or shopping as applicable.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive demand the immediate liquidation of accumulated cash advances and/or authorize the Municipal Treasurer to withhold salaries of those concerned active employees who would fail to liquidate within 30 days upon receipt of notice.

We have recommended that the Municipal School Board be reactivated to perform their duties and functions in accordance with Section 99 and 100 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in the formulation and approval of SEF Budget to attain its intended purpose.

We have recommended that the BAC ensure brand names, except when allowed, are not utilized as part of the description of the items to be procured as this violates Section 18 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We have recommended that the: 
The Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance Committee to initiate the drafting of an ordinance by the Sangguniang Bayan on the imposition of rental fees on the use of municipal facilities in line with the exercise of powers of the LGU to create 
sources of revenue; and 

Municipal Treasurer exert more efforts to enhance its revenue generating and collecting program as contained in the Municipal Revenue Code and other related ordinance to ensure that all collection estimates are fully collected.

We have recommended that management plan strategies by establishing timelines of procurement period on identified GAD projects and regular monitoring of accomplishments to ensure full implementation at yearend.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert best efforts to work back on the related records and transactions as well other payables to identify the cause of negative/abnormal balances and other reconciling items and effect necessary adjustments in order to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe prescribed regulations in rendering reports of financial transactions in accordance with Section 41 (Paragraph 2) of Presidential Decree No 1445 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the above disbursement vouchers to the Audit Team.

We have recommended that: 

The Local Finance committee include the collections from the rent income of Municipal Tractor and Ambulance in the source income under the appropriation ordinance as these are regular and ordinary source income of the Municipality; and 
 
The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant properly record collections from the rent income of Municipal Tractor and Ambulance in the General Fund Account and transfer the remaining balance of collections from the Trust Fund to the General Fund Account.

We have recommended the Municipal 
Accountant to: 

Record transactions within ten (10) days from receipt of the Disbursement Vouchers from the Treasury Office after which immediately submit to COA for post-audit.

We have recommended that Management : 

Demand from retired Municipal 
Treasurer Corazon Achivara, the settlement of her cash shortage amounting to ₱1,191,827.29 and ₱75,115.58 as Liquidating Officer and Collecting Officer, respectively;  and  to provide full accounting of the missing Accountable Forms. Do not issue her clearance from money and property accountability in case of failure to comply; and 
 
b. The Municipal Accountant Observe proper procedures in the recording of transactions, and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers per Accountable Officer to support balances of the Cash-in-vault.
We have recommended that management file appropriate legal actions against persons responsible for allowing expenditures for the office supplies in excess of its approved appropriations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare analysis of PPE accounts to determine the corresponding depreciation of all depreciable assets and effect the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide appropriate depreciation
expenses on all depreciable assets of the municipality and,
likewise, maintain updated Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards
(PPELC) as basis in computing depreciation in order to ensure fair
presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that:
a. the Municipal Accountant record accurately all transactions, particularly the
recognition of inventories and corresponding utilization/expense
portion, in the appropriate books of accounts in accordance with applicable
provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards;
b. the GSO or the MHO, as applicable, document all receipts and issues of
stocks and to regularly prepare and submit to the Accounting Office the
Report of Supplies and Materials Issued with supporting copies of Requisition
Issue Slips (RISs) for the recognition of expenses for the period; and
c. management provide a safe and adequate storage area for all supplies
carried in stock and allow significant increases in appropriation only upon
valid justification.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert best efforts to work
back on the related records and transactions as well other payables to
identify the cause of negative/abnormal balances and other reconciling items
and effect necessary adjustments in order to ensure fair presentation of
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly
observe prescribed regulations in rendering reports of financial
transactions in accordance with Section 41 (Paragraph 2) of
Presidential Decree No 1445 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No.
2009-006; and
b. the Municipal Accountant to submit the reports and accounts due for
submission as determined in Annex C of this report, otherwise, cause
the suspension of payment of his salaries until he shall have complied
with the requirements of the Commission or impose administrative
disciplinary action for the repeated or continued violation thereof
pursuant to Section 41 (Paragraph 3) of PD No 1445.

We recommended that management:
a. particularly the BAC, conduct all procurements in accordance with the
IRR of the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184) and other
applicable laws, rules and regulations; and
b. require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims are duly
supported in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 before
processing for payment.

We have recommended that the BAC conduct thorough bid evaluation in all
public bidding and that all required bid forms prescribed are complied by the
bidder, otherwise, it could be a ground for declaring the bidder as non-
responsive.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all
procurement of drugs and medicines shall comply with the PNF Manual and
Executive Order No. 49.

We recommended that Management:
a. withhold all appropriate taxes arising from government money payments;
and
b. ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted in accordance with BIR
Revenue Memorandum Circular No 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict
compliance to the recommendations on the budget review conducted by the
Sangguniang Panlalawigan to ensure that the concurred appropriation
ordinances are in compliance with existing rules and regulations particularly
Section 287 of the Local Government Code pertaining to the appropriation and
utilization of 20% of the Internal Revenue Allocation.

observations.managementAction:

Service Officer.

There were improvements observed in CY 2017, however, the Municipal

noted in the financial statements.

The Audit Team cannot consider the allegation of management that they failed to

Annual Investment Plan to fully and
effectively achieve the desired socio-
economic development and environmental
outcomes for the benefit of the
constituents.

observations.recommendation:

Local Chief Executive assign a
responsible person to ensure that
concerned officials comply with the
documentary requirements for the
contracts and purchase orders for
submission to the auditor within the
period prescribed by regulation to
avoid adverse audit and legal actions
that may arise from such failure.
observations.managementAction:
Majority of the copies of contracts were submitted only upon payment as
required by accounting unit. Almost all of the received copies of contracts were
furnished to the Audit Team beyond the prescribed period.
observations.reason:
Almost all of the received copies of contracts were furnished to the Audit

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

As head of the Internal Auditing Unit, the Municipal
Accountant should implement strong internal control over the
disbursements of government funds. Future disbursements
should only be recommended for approval by the head of
agency only if documentary requirements prescribed under
the above COA Circular were completely complied with.
observations.managementAction:
The accounting unit had implemented strict compliance as to
completeness of supporting documents prior to payment.

There were still transactions paid in CY 2016 without adequate
supporting documents that were communicated to management
thru the issuance of Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM)
as discussed in observation No. 5, Part II of this Report.

It should be the responsibility of the head of the accounting unit to
revalidate completion of supporting documents before submission
to the Audit Team.
observations.reason:
There were still transactions paid in CY 2016 without adequate supporting
documents despite improvements in compliance.

Reiterated in Observation No. 5, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:

Municipal Accountant:

a. Stop the practice of recording
outright expense to cash advances
Granted to officers and employees.
Succeeding grant of cash advances
should be recorded as “Advances to
Officers and Employees” and books
of accounts. Expenses shall only be
recognized upon submission of
liquidation report by the concerned
employee; and

b. Prepare the progress report of cash
advances of employees to be
submitted to the office for monitoring
and reference in post audit.
observations.managementAction:
Management justified that all cash advance granted in CY 2016 were recorded
properly as cash advance in the books of accounts.

Progress reports as of December 31, 2016 were already submitted to the
Audit Team.

Corresponding adjustments were effected.

Executive require the responsible
employees and officials to refund all

excess of allowable rates. Likewise,
lacking appropriate bills and receipts
should be submitted to support the
grant of actual expenditure otherwise,
these should be refunded.
observations.managementAction:
Management collected most of the travel allowance granted in excess of

Financial reports submitted show that there were refunds collected from officers
and employees in CY 2016.
observations.reason:
There were refunds collected but some minimal amounts remained for

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
management:
(a) Closely monitor the submission of liquidation papers
to immediately settle the accumulated cash advances;
(b) Comply strictly with the laws, rules and regulations
pertaining to cash advances in order to lessen the accumulation
of the same; and
(c) Demand the immediate liquidation of said cash
advances. Otherwise, the Municipal Treasurer, by the
authority of the Local Chief Executive (LCE) be authorized
to withhold salaries of those concerned who would fail to
liquidate within 30 days upon receipt of their demand letters.
observations.managementAction:
Progress Report on Cash Advances – General Fund as of December 31,
2016 was only submitted in April 2017 as the basis of accounting unit in
monitoring cash advances. This casts doubt that the monitoring of cash
advances was not properly undertaken in CY 2016.

Balance appearing in the Progress Report as of December 31, 2016 decreased
from (cid:1)2,595,398.00 to (cid:1)615,806.65 for the General Fund. There was no
Progress Report on Cash Advances submitted on Trust fund amounting to
(cid:1)1,772,410.00 as of December 31, 2016.

Liquidation of Cash Advances was demanded from persons accountable but the
withholding of salaries to settle outstanding cash advances was not implemented
by Management, thus, long over-due and outstanding cash advances still remain.
